name: June Analytics API
what_it_does: Provides a workspace-token-authenticated REST API and event tracking endpoint for June's product analytics platform, which is purpose-built for B2B SaaS companies to track user and company-level activation, retention, and churn metrics.
use_cases: Track server-side product events (e.g., feature used, report exported) with company and user context to populate June's B2B analytics dashboards
Identify and associate users to their companies so June can aggregate usage metrics at the account level for customer success reporting
Pull company feature adoption metrics to build a health scoring agent that surfaces at-risk accounts for the CS team
Send trait updates (plan type, MRR, employee count) to June user and company profiles to enable segmentation by business attributes
Automate churn signal detection by querying June's company activity data and triggering CRM alerts when an account goes dark
not_for: B2C consumer product analytics at scale — June is purpose-built for B2B SaaS; Amplitude or Mixpanel are better suited for consumer apps with millions of users
Detailed session replay or heatmap analysis — June focuses on aggregate behavioral metrics, not individual session recording
Real-time event streaming or sub-second analytics — June aggregates data for daily/weekly cadence product reviews, not real-time operational monitoring
best_when: A B2B SaaS product or CS agent needs to track company-level feature adoption, identify activation milestones, or surface churn risk signals from aggregated product usage data.
avoid_when: The analytics use case is consumer-focused, requires real-time event processing, or needs advanced funnel visualization and A/B testing beyond June's B2B-centric feature set.

auth.notes: Workspace token (write key) used for event tracking via the `/track`, `/identify`, and `/group` endpoints — passed as Basic auth username with empty password. A separate analytics API uses a different token for reading data. Tokens are per-workspace and have no granular scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan available with limited active companies tracked; API access available on all plans.
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro plan from $149/mo; Business plan pricing requires contacting sales. Limits scale with number of tracked companies.
pricing.notes: June's pricing model is based on the number of active companies tracked, not events or seats — well-suited for B2B SaaS where company count is bounded.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; designed for standard SaaS event volumes. Not suitable for extremely high-frequency event ingestion without contacting June support.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Event tracking endpoint returns 200 OK for all syntactically valid requests, even if semantic validation fails — silent data loss is possible. Analytics read API has clearer error responses.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Track events are not idempotent — duplicate calls create duplicate events. Identify and group calls are effectively idempotent as they overwrite trait values. Use a message_id field for deduplication where supported.
known_agent_gotchas: The write key (workspace token) and the analytics read key are different credentials — agents that conflate them will get authentication errors on read endpoints
Company (`group`) calls must be made before associating user events to a company — events sent without a prior group call may not roll up into company-level metrics
The track endpoint returns HTTP 200 for malformed payloads in some cases; agents should validate event schemas client-side before sending
June processes events asynchronously — newly ingested events do not appear in dashboards immediately; agents querying metrics right after ingestion will see stale data
Trait updates via `identify` completely overwrite the user profile if the same trait key is sent — agents must send the full desired trait set, not incremental patches

security_score_components.security_notes: Workspace token is a single shared secret with no scopes or rotation mechanism documented. Basic auth format (token as username) is standard for analytics SDKs. No IP allowlisting available.
